member.name: M:Foundatio.Caching.RedisCacheClient.SetAllInternalAsync(System.Collections.Generic.KeyValuePair{StackExchange.Redis.RedisKey,StackExchange.Redis.RedisValue}[],System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan})
member.summary: Internal method that performs the actual batch set operation. All keys in must be in the same hash slot for cluster mode.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Caching.RedisCacheClient.SupportsMsetexCommand
member.summary: Checks if the connected Redis server supports the MSETEX command (Redis 8.4+).

remarks.para: MSETEX allows setting multiple keys with expiration in a single atomic operation. StackExchange.Redis 2.10.1+ supports this via the StringSetAsync overload that accepts an parameter. However, SE.Redis does NOT automatically fall back to individual SET commands on older Redis versions - it will fail.
This method detects the Redis server version and caches the result. The cache is invalidated when the connection is restored (e.g., after failover to a different server).

Note: For batch sets WITHOUT expiration, use StringSetAsync(pairs, When, CommandFlags) which uses MSET - available since Redis 1.0.1 and doesn't require version detection. MSET replaces existing values and removes any existing TTL, just like regular SET.

member.returns: true if all connected primary servers support MSETEX; false if any primary is running Redis < 8.4 or no primaries are connected.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Caching.RedisCacheClient.NormalizeExpiration(System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan})
member.summary: Normalizes expiration: TimeSpan.MaxValue or overflow to DateTime.MaxValue means "no expiration".

member.name: M:Foundatio.Caching.RedisCacheClient.GetExpirationMilliseconds(System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan})
member.summary: Gets the expiration in milliseconds for use in Lua scripts. Returns null if no expiration should be set (null, TimeSpan.MaxValue, or overflow to DateTime.MaxValue).

member.name: P:Foundatio.Caching.RedisCacheClientOptions.ShouldThrowOnSerializationError
member.summary: Whether or not an error when deserializing a cache value should result in an exception being thrown or if it should just return an empty cache value

member.name: P:Foundatio.Caching.RedisCacheClientOptions.ReadMode
member.summary: Controls how read operations are routed in a master-replica topology. Set to to distribute reads to replica nodes (recommended for master-replica setups). Writes always go to the master regardless of this setting. Default is (reads go to master).

member.name: M:Foundatio.Redis.RedisValueExtensions.ToValueOfType``1(StackExchange.Redis.RedisValue,Foundatio.Serializer.ISerializer)
member.summary: Converts a to the specified type . Handles null Redis values, primitive types (bool, string, numeric), nullable numeric types, and falls back to the for complex types. Follows the same conversion strategy as TypeExtensions.ToType<T> in Foundatio core.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Redis.RedisValueExtensions.ToRedisValue``1(``0,Foundatio.Serializer.ISerializer)
member.summary: Converts a value of type to a . Null values are stored as a sentinel string ("@@NULL") to distinguish from missing keys. Handles primitive types directly and falls back to the serializer for complex types.

member.name: M:Foundatio.Redis.Utility.RedisPattern.Escape(System.String)
member.summary: Escapes Redis glob‑pattern meta characters so they are matched literally. Meta-chars (per KEYS/SCAN docs): *, ?, [, ], and \. See https://redis.io/docs/latest/commands/keys/ and https://redis.io/docs/latest/commands/scan/ for details.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Queues.RedisQueueOptions`1.ReadMode
member.summary:
Controls how read operations are routed in a master-replica topology. Set to to distribute reads to replica nodes. Writes always go to the master regardless of this setting. Default is (reads go to master).
Caution: Under very high throughput, a dequeue may fail to read a just-enqueued payload if replication hasn't caught up, which can cause message loss. Consider keeping the default for queues processing critical work items.

member.name: P:Foundatio.Storage.RedisFileStorageOptions.ReadMode
member.summary: Controls how read operations are routed in a master-replica topology. Set to to distribute reads to replica nodes. Writes always go to the master regardless of this setting. Default is (reads go to master).
